aliases: Baked Eggplant Pizza with Buffalo Mozzarella

english: Baked Eggplant Pizza with Buffalo Mozzarella

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 is the closest parent class for pizza as a food product. No specific subclass for Pizza alla Parmigiana with eggplant and buffalo mozzarella exists in FoodOn; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a composite dish category. No specific Parmigiana variant code exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Pizza alla Parmigiana; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a whole 12-inch pizza weighing approximately 450 g, typically shared between 2–3 people or consumed as a single large meal portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza, cheese, frozen, classic crust, cooked (SR Legacy) and Pizza, vegetable, regular crust (SR Legacy composite)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry for Pizza alla Parmigiana with buffalo mozzarella and baked eggplant exists. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza and vegetable pizza composite entries, cross-referenced with the input record per_100g values. Input values are consistent with USDA pizza composite ranges and have been retained where alignment is confirmed. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and extended micronutrients set to -1 where USDA composite data for this specific variant is not available at sufficient precision. GrabFood menu listing used only as a secondary cross-reference for portion weight; all nutrient values are grounded in USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Pizza alla Parmigiana with buffalo mozzarella and eggplant. GI of 80 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white-flour pizza base (GI ~80, glucose reference), which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor in this dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (80 × 85.05 g) / 100 = 68.0, where available carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 18.9 g/100g × 450 g / 100 = 85.05 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 400 mg/100 g; estimated 1800 mg per whole 12-inch pizza from dough salt, Parmesan, and tomato sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to 1–2 slices and request reduced salt in the dough and tomato sauce, as the sodium content of 400 mg/100 g (approximately 1800 mg per whole pizza) substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg per meal threshold for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined white flour base (GI ~80); carbohydrate 18.9 g/100 g with only 1.8 g/100 g dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit this pizza to 1–2 slices per sitting due to the high-GI refined wheat base (GI ~80) and low fibre-to-carbohydrate ratio, which promotes rapid postprandial glucose elevation; substituting a wholemeal or cauliflower base and pairing with a salad would meaningfully reduce glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.6 g/100 g from buffalo mozzarella and Parmesan; sodium 400 mg/100 g; trans fat 0.1 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of saturated fat at 5.6 g/100 g (25.2 g per whole pizza), sodium at 400 mg/100 g, and trace trans fat warrants portion restriction to 2 slices; olive oil and basil provide beneficial monounsaturated fats and polyphenols that partially offset cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (eggplant, tomato sauce); saturated fat 5.6 g/100 g exceeding 5 g/100 g RA threshold; refined carbohydrate-driven glycaemic load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables should avoid this pizza due to eggplant and tomato sauce content; saturated fat at 5.6 g/100 g marginally exceeds the RA threshold and may promote TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, and a wholemeal base with reduced cheese quantity is recommended if consumed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 12-inch Pizza alla Parmigiana (Vegetarian) is an Italian-style baked pizza served in Singapore, featuring a hand-stretched refined wheat flour base topped with baked eggplant (aubergine) slices, rich tomato sauce, creamy buffalo mozzarella, grated Parmesan cheese, and fresh basil leaves. The pizza is baked at high temperature until the crust is golden and crisp at the edges with a soft, chewy interior. The flavour profile is savoury and umami-rich, with the sweetness of slow-cooked tomato sauce balanced by the saltiness of aged Parmesan and the milky creaminess of buffalo mozzarella. The eggplant contributes a soft, slightly smoky texture. Per 100 g, this pizza provides approximately 233 kcal, 11.1 g protein, 12.2 g fat (5.6 g saturated), 18.9 g carbohydrate, 1.8 g dietary fibre, and 400 mg sodium. The high sodium content (400 mg/100 g) and high-GI refined flour base (GI ~80) make this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan, halal-unverified, and contains gluten and dairy allergens. A whole 12-inch pizza weighs approximately 450 g and is suitable as a shared meal for 2–3 people or a single large meal portion. The dish is culturally associated with Southern Italian cuisine, specifically the Campania and Sicily regions where melanzane alla parmigiana (eggplant Parmesan) is a traditional preparation, adapted here as a pizza topping. In Singapore, this pizza is available through Italian restaurant chains and independent pizzerias listed on food delivery platforms.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a 12-inch Parmigiana pizza?
Is this pizza suitable for diabetics?

What allergens are in Pizza alla Parmigiana?

Is this pizza high in saturated fat?

What is the GI of pizza with white flour base?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza composites and cross-referenced with the input GrabFood record (confidence 0.7). No exact USDA entry for Pizza alla Parmigiana with buffalo mozzarella and eggplant exists; values are consistent with USDA cheese and vegetable pizza composites but represent an adaptation rather than an exact match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white-flour pizza base.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific pizza variant (buffalo mozzarella + eggplant + Parmesan composite) is not available at sufficient precision in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza entries as of 2024. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite pizza variant not reported at sufficient precision in the USDA pizza composite entries consulted. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not available at sufficient precision for this specific pizza composite in USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. Glycemic load per serving is 68.0 (GL = GI/100 × available carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 0.80 × 85.05 g = 68.0). This dish is served in Singapore by GrabFood-listed Italian restaurants; the dish style originates from Italy (Campania/Sicily region). Halal status is unverified and not assumed. No zero-value core macros are present in this record. Local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided as transliterations and semantic translations appropriate for the Singapore market context, as this Italian dish is sold in Singapore. Aliases self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
